task.title: Add advisory code-comment/docstring drift prompt and false-positive labels.

context.problem_statement: Inline comments and docstrings can become stale in the same way as docs, but AGW-031 should keep this as an advisory review signal with explicit false-positive handling rather than a blocking CI gate or scanner.
background: workflows/prompts/personas/doc-code-delta.md already includes in-code comments that describe behavior or constraints in its review scope.
workflows/prompts/templates/review-finding.md and workflows/prompts/review-synthesis.md already require confidence, disposition, and false-positive notes.
AGW-044 already added formal comment-only receipt evidence for learning-comments mode.
AGW-078 already expanded learning-comments for sparse maintained comments and non-developer explanation notes.
AGW-030 added an opt-in docs-as-tests experiment in repo-contract-kit; AGW-031 should stay prompt/output-label focused in agent-workflow-kit.
non_goals: Do not implement a static stale-comment scanner, parser, or blocking CI gate.
Do not auto-edit source comments or docstrings.
Do not change learning-comments receipt validation or comment-only proof semantics from AGW-044.
Do not modify repo-contract-kit templates, installed Make targets, or kit release files.
Do not treat every stale-looking comment as a defect; low-confidence cases must remain advisory, rejected, deferred, or explicitly false-positive-labeled.
Do not add network, browser, hosted model, or GitHub API behavior.

report_sources: source and kit remote refs checked after AGW-030 closeout
finalizer_receipt_paths: source main commit d594e7028ec1dc69cf645b2437d5bbea129671d1 closed AGW-030
repo-contract-kit main commit c12c19c3d0fe2b5744c22b07a371a1e8728b4674 completed repo-contract-kit 0.4.52
previous_task_state.notes: AGW-031 packet creation began with a clean source checkout, source task-status reporting zero active tasks and no hazards, and make agent-next selecting AGW-031.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: AGW-030 has been implemented, validated, pushed in repo-contract-kit, and source-closed; source task-status reports no active, stale, hazardous, or unknown-scope tasks.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with exactly one AGW-031 worker in agent-workflow-kit; refresh git status and task-status if the checkout becomes dirty before worker launch.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep agent-workflow-kit as the canonical prompt, schema, fixture, and workflow source for local-first review agents and their generated Codex adapters.

area_contracts.purpose: Documentation/code delta reviewer prompt that already compares docs, examples, comments, runbooks, and workflows against implementation evidence.
Shared review finding format with priority, area, confidence, disposition, evidence, recommendation, verification, and false-positive check.
Synthesis prompt that merges findings, records dispositions, suppresses nits, and preserves false-positive notes.
Machine-readable synthesis contract for findings, dispositions, and false-positive notes.
Deterministic prompt-output regression fixtures for schema handling, finding quality, false-positive notes, and duplicate discipline.

stop_conditions: Stop if the implementation requires repo-contract-kit installer or target-repo managed-file changes.
Stop if output labels would require a breaking schema migration rather than additive optional fields or prompt guidance.
Stop if the design would make comment/docstring drift a default blocker or CI failure.
Stop if the worker cannot define evidence requirements that distinguish stale comments from plausible false positives.
Stop if validation reveals generated adapter drift or prompt fixture failures outside the AGW-031 scope.

protected_files: repo-contract-kit managed templates or installed target-repo files
learning-comments receipt validation unless a validation failure proves a direct prompt-adapter bug
runtime review runner behavior unless fixture validation cannot represent the new labels without a narrow additive check
unrelated backlog rows, unrelated prompt personas, and unrelated dirty work
expected_outputs: Prompt guidance that treats comment and docstring drift as advisory documentation hygiene unless concrete implementation or public behavior evidence makes it higher severity.
Evidence rules requiring reviewers to cite the stale comment/docstring and the current implementation, tests, docs, ADR, or runtime behavior that contradicts it.

Output labels or finding fields that distinguish comment-drift, docstring-drift, stale-comment, misleading-comment, stale-docstring, intentionally-stable-comment, generated-or-vendored-comment, and low-confidence/speculative drift cases.
False-positive handling that tells agents when to reject, defer, duplicate, or record a harmless explanation instead of opening a finding.
Prompt regression fixture coverage or equivalent focused tests that prevent the new labels and false-positive notes from being dropped.
Generated Codex prompt adapters refreshed from workflows/prompts.
Docs and changelog/version updates for the prompt-output contract change.
Backlog and summary closeout only after validation passes.

acceptance_criteria.description: The doc-code-delta prompt explicitly reviews behavior-describing comments and docstrings for drift, while keeping low-confidence cases advisory and non-blocking.
The finding template or synthesis guidance names labels for comment/docstring drift and false-positive categories without making schema-breaking changes.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review workflows/prompts/templates/review-finding.md, workflows/prompts/review-synthesis.md, and schemas/review-synthesis.schema.json if touched.

acceptance_criteria.description: Every comment/docstring drift finding requires concrete evidence from both the maintained comment/docstring and the current source of truth.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Prompt review plus prompt-regression fixture or focused test evidence.

acceptance_criteria.description: False-positive labels cover generated/vendor code, intentional historical notes, examples or docs intentionally not mirroring implementation internals, framework convention comments, and speculative or low-confidence drift.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review prompt/template text and fixture expectations.

acceptance_criteria.description: The change does not add a scanner, CI gate, repo-contract-kit target, or automatic source comment edits.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run git diff --name-only and inspect changed files.

acceptance_criteria.description: Prompt adapters are regenerated and checked.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 make prompt-adapters-export and PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 make prompt-adapters-check.

acceptance_criteria.description: Prompt regression fixtures or equivalent tests pass and include at least one advisory comment/docstring drift case with false-positive handling.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_prompt_regression_fixtures and PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 scripts/run_prompt_regression_fixtures.py.

acceptance_criteria.description: Docs impact is handled in user-facing prompt docs and version/changelog bookkeeping.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run docs checks, make version-check, and inspect changed docs.

commands.expected_result: pass and refresh generated .codex prompt adapters
pass; pre-existing instruction hygiene warning may be reported if unchanged
pass after a patch bump and changelog entry

evidence_to_capture: Diff summary and changed-file list proving source-side prompt/docs/fixture scope.

readiness_check.command: make agent-task-ready TASK=AGW-031 TASK_READY_JSON=1 or record why unavailable in the source parent run
readiness_check.expected_result: ready report passes or a blocker is recorded before implementation starts

lifecycle_action.command: make agent-task-finalize TASK=AGW-031 TASK_RECEIPT=<path> TASK_FINALIZE_JSON=1 or lifecycle fallback
lifecycle_action.expected_result: metadata is closed and final receipt is linked, or fallback receipt is durable

final_task_status.expected_result: task is terminal or no local metadata was created and the reason is recorded

closeout_preview.expected_result: eligible, retained, or blocked cleanup state is recorded

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether the checkout is clean, only expected files are dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This changes user-facing prompt behavior and the review-output labeling contract.

known_risks: Agents may over-report harmless comments as defects if evidence thresholds and false-positive labels are vague.
Docstrings can be part of public API documentation in some languages, so severity must depend on concrete user-facing impact.
Prompt labels can drift from schema/fixture validation if only prose changes are made.
The task can expand into scanner or CI-gate work unless the worker preserves the advisory-only boundary.
stop_conditions: The implementation needs repo-contract-kit or installed target-repo changes.
The worker cannot add labels without a breaking schema change.
Validation shows prompt adapters cannot be regenerated cleanly.
A proposed change would make comment/docstring drift blocking by default.

approval.notes: Active user goal is to add fully planned backlog packets and implement remaining workflow-stack items serially with one worker per backlog item.

token_budget.guidance: Use the inspect_first list before broad repo reads; this is a prompt/template/fixture slice, not a kit or scanner implementation.

dependencies: AGW-044 comment-only receipt proof is already separate and should not be changed.
AGW-078 learning-comments explainability work is already separate and should not be reworked.
Generated .codex adapters must come from make prompt-adapters-export.
handoff.next_packet_hint: After AGW-031 closes, continue with the next source-side AGW item selected by make agent-next, likely AGW-048 unless priority order changes.
